title: pilosella densiflora subsp. bauhinifolia
intro: Pilosella densiflora subsp. bauhinifolia, also known as Alpine Mouse-ear or a subspecies of the Rock Cinquefoil, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. Traditionally, Pilosella densiflora subsp. bauhinifolia has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Pilosella densiflora subsp. bauhinifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its potential medicinal properties to be harnessed in various forms.
